SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",
    COUNT(*) AS "View Count",
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $1 THEN $2
        WHEN $3 THEN $4
        WHEN $5 THEN $6
        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $12::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $13
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
) view_classification
GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19
        WHEN $20 THEN $21
        WHEN $22 THEN $23
        WHEN $24 THEN $25
        ELSE $26
    END

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.view_politician_risk_summary AS
 WITH party_ballot_consensus AS (
         SELECT vd.embedded_id_ballot_id,
            vd.party,
            mode() WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY UPPER((vd.vote)::text)) AS party_consensus_vote
           FROM public.vote_data vd
          WHERE UPPER((vd.vote)::text) <> 'FRÅNVARANDE'
            AND vd.party IS NOT NULL
            AND vd.vote_date >= (CURRENT_DATE - '2 years'::interval)
          GROUP BY vd.embedded_id_ballot_id, vd.party
        ), politician_vote_metrics AS (
         SELECT p.id AS person_id,
            count(DISTINCT vd.embedded_id_ballot_id) AS total_votes,
            count(DISTINCT vd.embedded_id_ballot_id) FILTER (
                WHERE UPPER((vd.vote)::text) = 'FRÅNVARANDE'
            ) AS absent_votes,
            count(DISTINCT vd.embedded_id_ballot_id) FILTER (
                WHERE pbc.party_consensus_vote IS NOT NULL
                  AND UPPER((vd.vote)::text) <> pbc.party_consensus_vote
                  AND UPPER((vd.vote)::text) <> 'FRÅNVARANDE'
            ) AS rebel_votes
           FROM public.person_data p
             LEFT JOIN public.vote_data vd
               ON (vd.embedded_id_intressent_id)::text = (p.id)::text
              AND vd.vote_date >= (CURRENT_DATE - '2 years'::interval)
             LEFT JOIN party_ballot_consensus pbc
               ON (pbc.embedded_id_ballot_id)::text = (vd.embedded_id_ballot_id)::text
              AND (pbc.party)::text = (vd.party)::text
          WHERE (p.status)::text = ANY (ARRAY[
              'Tjänstgörande riksdagsledamot'::text,
              'Tjänstgörande ersättare'::text,
              'Tillgänglig ersättare'::text])
          GROUP BY p.id
        ), politician_document_metrics AS (
         SELECT dpr.person_reference_id,
            count(DISTINCT dsc.hjid) AS documents_last_year
           FROM public.document_status_container dsc
             LEFT JOIN public.document_data dd ON (dsc.document_document_status_con_0)::text = (dd.id)::text
             LEFT JOIN public.document_person_reference_co_0 dprc ON dsc.hjid = dprc.hjid
             LEFT JOIN public.document_person_reference_da_0 dpr ON dpr.document_person_reference_li_1 = dprc.hjid
          WHERE dd.made_public_date >= (CURRENT_DATE - '1 year'::interval)
            AND dpr.person_reference_id IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY dpr.person_reference_id
        ), risk_calculations AS (
         SELECT p.id AS person_id,
            p.first_name, p.last_name, p.party, p.status,
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) AS total_violations,
            max(rv.detected_date) AS latest_violation_date,
            -- Map to actual DRL rule_group values (Behavior, Assignments, Experience, Party, Role, Attribute)
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) FILTER (WHERE (rv.rule_group)::text = 'Behavior') AS behavior_violations,
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) FILTER (WHERE (rv.rule_group)::text = 'Assignments') AS assignments_violations,
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) FILTER (WHERE (rv.rule_group)::text = 'Experience') AS experience_violations,
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) FILTER (WHERE (rv.rule_group)::text = 'Party') AS party_violations,
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) FILTER (WHERE (rv.rule_group)::text = 'Role') AS role_violations,
            COALESCE(round(pvm.absent_votes::numeric / NULLIF(pvm.total_votes, 0)::numeric * 100, 2), 0::numeric) AS annual_absence_rate,
            COALESCE(round(pvm.rebel_votes::numeric / NULLIF(pvm.total_votes, 0)::numeric * 100, 2), 0::numeric) AS annual_rebel_rate,
            COALESCE(pvm.total_votes, 0::bigint) AS annual_vote_count,
            COALESCE(pdm.documents_last_year, 0::bigint) AS documents_last_year,
            ((LEAST(count(DISTINCT rv.id) * 2, 40::bigint))::numeric
             + (COALESCE(round(pvm.absent_votes::numeric / NULLIF(pvm.total_votes, 0)::numeric * 100, 2), 0::numeric) * 30 / 100.0)
             + (COALESCE(round(pvm.rebel_votes::numeric / NULLIF(pvm.total_votes, 0)::numeric * 100, 2), 0::numeric) * 20 / 100.0)
             + (CASE WHEN COALESCE(pdm.documents_last_year, 0::bigint) < 5 THEN 10 ELSE 0 END)::numeric
            ) AS calculated_risk_score
           FROM public.person_data p
             LEFT JOIN politician_vote_metrics pvm ON (pvm.person_id)::text = (p.id)::text
             LEFT JOIN politician_document_metrics pdm ON (pdm.person_reference_id)::text = (p.id)::text
             LEFT JOIN public.rule_violation rv
               ON (rv.reference_id)::text = (p.id)::text
              AND (rv.resource_type)::text = 'POLITICIAN'
              AND (rv.status)::text = ANY (ARRAY['MINOR'::text, 'MAJOR'::text, 'CRITICAL'::text])
          WHERE (p.status)::text = ANY (ARRAY[
              'Tjänstgörande riksdagsledamot'::text,
              'Tjänstgörande ersättare'::text,
              'Tillgänglig ersättare'::text])
          GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name, p.party, p.status,
                   pvm.absent_votes, pvm.total_votes, pvm.rebel_votes, pdm.documents_last_year
        )
 SELECT person_id, first_name, last_name, party, status,
    total_violations, latest_violation_date,
    -- Column name backward compatibility mapping (actual DRL rule_group → legacy column name):
    -- 'Behavior' (PoliticianLazy, HighRebelRate, LowEngagement) → absenteeism_violations
    -- 'Experience' (MinisterWithoutParliamentExperience) → effectiveness_violations
    -- 'Assignments' (BalancedRules: multiple assignments) → discipline_violations
    -- 'Party' (LeftPartyStillHoldingPositions) → productivity_violations
    -- 'Role' (Speaker, LeadershipRole) → collaboration_violations
    behavior_violations AS absenteeism_violations,
    experience_violations AS effectiveness_violations,
    assignments_violations AS discipline_violations,
    party_violations AS productivity_violations,
    role_violations AS collaboration_violations,
    annual_absence_rate, annual_rebel_rate, annual_vote_count, documents_last_year,
    round(calculated_risk_score, 2) AS risk_score,
    CASE
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 65 THEN 'CRITICAL'
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 45 THEN 'HIGH'
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 25 THEN 'MEDIUM'
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 10 THEN 'LOW'
        ELSE 'MINIMAL'
    END AS risk_level,
    CASE
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 65 THEN 'Critical risk politician - immediate investigation required'
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 45 THEN 'High risk politician - performance concerns warrant review'
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 25 THEN 'Moderate risk - monitor for declining performance'
        WHEN calculated_risk_score >= 10 THEN 'Low risk - performing within acceptable standards'
        ELSE 'Standard risk profile'
    END AS risk_assessment
   FROM risk_calculations
  ORDER BY calculated_risk_score DESC

CREATE VIEW public.view_election_cycle_comparative_analysis AS
 WITH v151_base AS (
         WITH election_cycle_periods AS (
                 SELECT ((((1994)::numeric + (floor((((year_series.year_series - 1994))::numeric / 4.0)) * (4)::numeric)) || '-'::text) || (((1994)::numeric + (floor((((year_series.year_series - 1994))::numeric / 4.0)) * (4)::numeric)) + (4)::numeric)) AS election_cycle_id,
                    (((year_series.year_series)::numeric - ((1994)::numeric + (floor((((year_series.year_series - 1994))::numeric / 4.0)) * (4)::numeric))) + (1)::numeric) AS cycle_year,
                    year_series.year_series AS calendar_year
                   FROM generate_series(1994, ((EXTRACT(year FROM CURRENT_DATE))::integer + 4), 1) year_series(year_series)
                )
         SELECT ecp.election_cycle_id,
            ecp.cycle_year,
            ecp.calendar_year,
            'annual'::text AS semester,
            ppm.party,
            max(ppm.performance_score) AS performance_score,
            max(ppm.active_members) AS active_members,
            max(ppm.total_violations) AS party_violations,
            round(avg(ppm.avg_win_rate), 2) AS party_win_rate,
            round(avg(ppm.avg_participation_rate), 2) AS party_participation_rate,
            max(ppm.documents_last_year) AS documents_last_year,
            round(avg(ppm.avg_rebel_rate), 2) AS party_avg_rebel_rate,
            count(DISTINCT cpm.committee_code) AS committees_active
           FROM ((election_cycle_periods ecp
             CROSS JOIN public.view_party_performance_metrics ppm)
             LEFT JOIN public.view_committee_productivity_matrix cpm ON ((EXTRACT(year FROM cpm.period_start) = (ecp.calendar_year)::numeric)))
          WHERE (ppm.party IS NOT NULL)
          GROUP BY ecp.election_cycle_id, ecp.cycle_year, ecp.calendar_year, ppm.party
          ORDER BY ecp.election_cycle_id, ecp.cycle_year, 'annual'::text, ppm.party
        ), windowed AS (
         SELECT v.election_cycle_id,
            v.cycle_year,
            v.calendar_year,
            v.semester,
            v.party,
            v.performance_score,
            v.active_members,
            v.party_violations,
            v.party_win_rate,
            v.party_participation_rate,
            v.documents_last_year,
            v.party_avg_rebel_rate,
            v.committees_active,
            rank() OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id ORDER BY v.performance_score DESC NULLS LAST) AS rank_by_performance,
            rank() OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id ORDER BY v.party_avg_rebel_rate) AS rank_by_discipline,
            percent_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id ORDER BY v.performance_score DESC NULLS LAST) AS percent_rank_performance,
            ntile(4) OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id ORDER BY v.performance_score DESC NULLS LAST) AS ntile_party_tier,
            lag(v.performance_score) OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id, v.party ORDER BY v.cycle_year) AS prev_cycle_performance,
            lag(v.documents_last_year) OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id, v.party ORDER BY v.cycle_year) AS prev_cycle_documents,
            stddev_pop(v.performance_score) OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id) AS stddev_performance,
            count(*) OVER (PARTITION BY v.election_cycle_id) AS cycle_party_count
           FROM v151_base v
        )
 SELECT election_cycle_id,
    cycle_year,
    calendar_year,
    semester,
    party,
    performance_score,
    active_members,
    party_violations,
    party_win_rate,
    party_participation_rate,
    documents_last_year,
    party_avg_rebel_rate,
    committees_active,
    rank_by_performance,
    rank_by_discipline,
    percent_rank_performance,
    ntile_party_tier,
    prev_cycle_performance,
    prev_cycle_documents,
    stddev_performance,
    cycle_party_count,
        CASE
            WHEN ((prev_cycle_performance IS NOT NULL) AND (prev_cycle_performance > (0)::numeric)) THEN round((((performance_score - prev_cycle_performance) / prev_cycle_performance) * (100)::numeric), 2)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END AS change_performance_pct,
        CASE
            WHEN ((prev_cycle_documents IS NOT NULL) AND (prev_cycle_documents > 0)) THEN round(((((documents_last_year - prev_cycle_documents))::numeric / (prev_cycle_documents)::numeric) * (100)::numeric), 2)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END AS change_documents_pct,
        CASE
            WHEN (prev_cycle_performance IS NULL) THEN 'baseline'::text
            WHEN (performance_score > (prev_cycle_performance + (10)::numeric)) THEN 'improving'::text
            WHEN (performance_score < (prev_cycle_performance - (10)::numeric)) THEN 'declining'::text
            ELSE 'stable'::text
        END AS performance_trend,
    round((((performance_score * 0.4) + (party_win_rate * 0.3)) + (party_participation_rate * 0.3)), 2) AS discipline_score,
    round((((rank_by_performance)::numeric / (NULLIF(cycle_party_count, 0))::numeric) * (100)::numeric), 2) AS competitiveness_index
   FROM windowed w

SELECT * FROM (SELECT current_database() AS current_database, n.nspname,c.relname,a.attname,a.atttypid,a.attnotnull  OR (t.typtype = $4 AND t.typnotnull) AS attnotnull,a.atttypmod,a.attlen,t.typtypmod,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY a.attrelid ORDER BY a.attnum) AS attnum, nullif(a.attidentity, $5) as attidentity,nullif(a.attgenerated, $6) as attgenerated,pg_catalog.pg_get_expr(def.adbin, def.adrelid) AS adsrc,dsc.description,t.typbasetype,t.typtype  FROM pg_catalog.pg_namespace n  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class c ON (c.relnamespace = n.oid)  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attribute a ON (a.attrelid=c.oid)  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_type t ON (a.atttypid = t.oid)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attrdef def ON (a.attrelid=def.adrelid AND a.attnum = def.adnum)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description dsc ON (c.oid=dsc.objoid AND a.attnum = dsc.objsubid)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class dc ON (dc.oid=dsc.classoid AND dc.relname=$7)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace dn ON (dc.relnamespace=dn.oid AND dn.nspname=$8)  WHERE c.relkind in ($9,$10,$11,$12,$13) and a.attnum > $14 AND NOT a.attisdropped  AND n.nspname LIKE $1 AND c.relname LIKE $2) c WHERE $15  AND attname LIKE $3 ORDER BY nspname,c.relname,attnum

CREATE VIEW public.view_riksdagen_party_transition_history AS
 WITH known_parties AS (
     -- Authoritative party codes from sweden_political_party table
     SELECT short_code FROM public.sweden_political_party
     WHERE short_code IS NOT NULL AND short_code <> '' AND short_code <> '-'
     UNION
     -- Historical party codes found in person_data.party but not in
     -- sweden_political_party (source: Riksdagen open data API person records)
     SELECT unnest(ARRAY['FP','NYD','VPK','K','H','BF','KrVP']) AS short_code
 ),
 party_name_map AS (
     -- Official Swedish party name changes (source: Swedish Parliament records)
     -- Maps historical codes to their modern equivalents to distinguish
     -- party RENAMES from genuine political SWITCHES
     -- Note: Historical codes kept in SQL rather than sweden_political_party
     -- table to avoid side effects on other views that use that table
     SELECT * FROM (VALUES
         ('FP', 'L'),       -- Folkpartiet → Liberalerna (renamed 2015)
         ('VPK', 'V'),      -- Vänsterpartiet kommunisterna → Vänsterpartiet (1990)
         ('K - VPK', 'V'),  -- Kommunisterna → VPK → Vänsterpartiet
         ('K', 'V'),        -- Kommunisterna (historical)
         ('BF - C', 'C'),   -- Bondeförbundet → Centerpartiet (renamed 1957)
         ('H - M', 'M')     -- Högerpartiet → Moderaterna (renamed 1969)
     ) AS t(old_code, new_code)
 ),
 assignment_party_periods AS (
     SELECT a.intressent_id AS person_id,
            a.org_code AS party,
            a.from_date,
            COALESCE(a.to_date, CURRENT_DATE) AS to_date,
            a.assignment_type,
            a.role_code,
            a.status
       FROM public.assignment_data a
      WHERE a.org_code IN (SELECT short_code FROM known_parties)
        AND a.from_date IS NOT NULL
        AND a.from_date >= '1990-01-01'::date
 ),
 person_party_timeline AS (
     SELECT person_id,
            party,
            min(from_date) AS first_date,
            max(to_date) AS last_date
       FROM assignment_party_periods
      GROUP BY person_id, party
 ),
 transitions_raw AS (
     SELECT t1.person_id,
            t1.party AS previous_party,
            t2.party AS new_party,
            t2.first_date AS transition_date
       FROM person_party_timeline t1
       JOIN person_party_timeline t2
         ON t1.person_id = t2.person_id
        AND t1.party <> t2.party
        AND t2.first_date > t1.first_date
        AND NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM person_party_timeline t3
             WHERE t3.person_id = t1.person_id
               AND t3.party = t2.party
               AND t3.first_date > t1.first_date
               AND t3.first_date < t2.first_date
        )
     WHERE COALESCE((SELECT pm.new_code FROM party_name_map pm WHERE pm.old_code = t1.party), t1.party)
        <> COALESCE((SELECT pm.new_code FROM party_name_map pm WHERE pm.old_code = t2.party), t2.party)
 ),
 vote_party_periods AS (
     SELECT vd.embedded_id_intressent_id AS person_id,
            vd.party,
            min(vd.vote_date) AS first_vote_date,
            max(vd.vote_date) AS last_vote_date
       FROM public.vote_data vd
      WHERE vd.party IS NOT NULL
        AND vd.party <> '-'
        AND vd.party <> ''
        AND vd.vote_date IS NOT NULL
        AND vd.vote_date >= '2002-01-01'::date
      GROUP BY vd.embedded_id_intressent_id, vd.party
     HAVING count(*) >= 3
 ),
 vote_transitions AS (
     SELECT v1.person_id,
            v1.party AS previous_party,
            v2.party AS new_party,
            v2.first_vote_date AS transition_date
       FROM vote_party_periods v1
       JOIN vote_party_periods v2
         ON v1.person_id = v2.person_id
        AND v1.party <> v2.party
        AND v2.first_vote_date > v1.last_vote_date
        AND NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM vote_party_periods v3
             WHERE v3.person_id = v1.person_id
               AND v3.first_vote_date > v1.last_vote_date
               AND v3.first_vote_date < v2.first_vote_date
               AND v3.party <> v1.party
               AND v3.party <> v2.party
        )
 ),
 all_transitions AS (
     SELECT person_id, previous_party, new_party, transition_date FROM transitions_raw
     UNION
     SELECT person_id, previous_party, new_party, transition_date FROM vote_transitions
 ),
 deduplicated AS (
     SELECT person_id, previous_party, new_party, min(transition_date) AS transition_date
       FROM all_transitions
      GROUP BY person_id, previous_party, new_party
 ),
 election_proximity AS (
     SELECT unnest(ARRAY[
         '2002-09-15'::date, '2006-09-17'::date, '2010-09-19'::date,
         '2014-09-14'::date, '2018-09-09'::date, '2022-09-11'::date,
         '2026-09-13'::date
     ]) AS election_date
 )
 SELECT dt.person_id,
    p.first_name,
    p.last_name,
    dt.previous_party,
    dt.new_party,
    dt.transition_date,
    CASE
        WHEN (SELECT pm.new_code FROM party_name_map pm WHERE pm.old_code = dt.previous_party) =
             COALESCE((SELECT pm.new_code FROM party_name_map pm WHERE pm.old_code = dt.new_party), dt.new_party)
        THEN 'PARTY_RENAMED'::text
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM public.vote_data vd
             WHERE (vd.embedded_id_intressent_id)::text = (dt.person_id)::text
               AND vd.vote_date >= (dt.transition_date - '60 days'::interval)
               AND vd.vote_date < dt.transition_date
        ) THEN 'SWITCHED_WHILE_SERVING'::text
        ELSE 'REJOINED_RIKSDAGEN'::text
    END AS transition_type,
    EXTRACT(year FROM dt.transition_date)::integer AS transition_year,
    ( SELECT min(ed.election_date)
           FROM election_proximity ed
          WHERE ed.election_date > dt.transition_date) AS next_election,
    (EXTRACT(year FROM age(
        (( SELECT min(ed.election_date) FROM election_proximity ed
           WHERE ed.election_date > dt.transition_date))::timestamp with time zone,
        dt.transition_date::timestamp with time zone)) * 12 +
     EXTRACT(month FROM age(
        (( SELECT min(ed.election_date) FROM election_proximity ed
           WHERE ed.election_date > dt.transition_date))::timestamp with time zone,
        dt.transition_date::timestamp with time zone)))::integer AS months_until_next_election,
    ( SELECT max(ed.election_date)
           FROM election_proximity ed
          WHERE ed.election_date < dt.transition_date) AS previous_election,
    (EXTRACT(year FROM age(
        dt.transition_date::timestamp with time zone,
        (( SELECT max(ed.election_date) FROM election_proximity ed
           WHERE ed.election_date < dt.transition_date))::timestamp with time zone)) * 12 +
     EXTRACT(month FROM age(
        dt.transition_date::timestamp with time zone,
        (( SELECT max(ed.election_date) FROM election_proximity ed
           WHERE ed.election_date < dt.transition_date))::timestamp with time zone)))::integer AS months_since_last_election
   FROM deduplicated dt
   JOIN public.person_data p ON (dt.person_id)::text = (p.id)::text
  ORDER BY dt.transition_date DESC

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
      -- Skip views that timed out or had errors during Phase 1
      -- row_count: -1 = timeout, -2 = error, 0+ = success
      AND row_count >= $4
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL views, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE vc.row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty views
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($5, $6) THEN $7
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($8, $9, $10) THEN $11
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($12, $13, $14) THEN $15
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $16 THEN $17
            ELSE $18
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $19
      AND c.data_type IN ($20, $21, $22)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($23, $24) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $25::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($26,
                                   $27,
                                   $28) THEN
                LEAST($29::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $31 THEN 
                LEAST($32::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $34 THEN 
                LEAST($35::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $38 THEN 
                LEAST($39::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $40 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $41 THEN 
                LEAST($42::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $43 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $44 THEN 
                LEAST($45::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $47 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $48 THEN 
                LEAST($49::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($50::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $51::int THEN $52
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($53,
                                   $54,
                                   $55) THEN $56
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $57 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $58 THEN $59
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $60 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $61 THEN $62
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $64 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $65 THEN $66
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $67 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $68 THEN $69
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $70 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $71 THEN $72
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $73 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $74 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $75 THEN $76
            ELSE $77
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $78 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $79 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $80 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $81 || $82 ||
                $83 ||
                $84 ||
                $85 ||
                $86 ||
                $87 ||
                $88 ||
                $89 ||
                $90 ||
                $91 || $92 ||
                $93 || $94 || $95 || $96 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $97 THEN $98 || $99
                    ELSE $100 || $101
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $102 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $103 || $104 ||
                $105 ||
                $106 ||
                $107 ||
                $108 ||
                $109 ||
                $110 ||
                $111 ||
                $112 ||
                $113 || $114 ||
                $115 || $116 || $117 || $118 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $119 THEN $120 || $121
                    ELSE $122 || $123
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $124 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $125 || $126 ||
                $127 ||
                $128 ||
                $129 ||
                $130 ||
                $131 ||
                $132 ||
                $133 ||
                $134 ||
                $135 || $136 ||
                $137 || $138 || $139 || $140 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $141 THEN $142 || $143
                    ELSE $144 || $145
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $146 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $147 || $148 ||
                $149 ||
                $150 ||
                $151 ||
                $152 ||
                $153 ||
                $154 ||
                $155 ||
                $156 || $157 ||
                $158 || $159 || $160 || $161 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $162 THEN $163 || $164
                    ELSE $165 || $166
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $167 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $168 || $169 ||
                $170 ||
                $171 ||
                $172 ||
                $173 ||
                $174 ||
                $175 ||
                $176 ||
                $177 ||
                $178 || $179 ||
                $180 || $181 || $182 || $183 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $184 THEN $185 || $186
                    ELSE $187 || $188
                END,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $189 || $190 ||
                $191 || $192 ||
                $193 || $194 || $195 || $196 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $197 THEN $198 || $199
                    ELSE $200 || $201
                END,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $202 THEN $203
        WHEN $204 THEN $205
        WHEN $206 THEN $207
        WHEN $208 THEN $209
        WHEN $210 THEN $211
        ELSE $212
    END,
    viewname

CREATE VIEW public.view_party_performance_metrics AS
 WITH party_member_counts AS (
         SELECT person_data.party,
            count(*) FILTER (WHERE (person_data.status)::text = ANY (ARRAY[
                'Tjänstgörande riksdagsledamot'::text,
                'Tjänstgörande ersättare'::text,
                'Tillgänglig ersättare'::text])) AS active_members,
            count(*) FILTER (WHERE (person_data.status)::text LIKE 'Tidigare%'
                OR (person_data.status)::text = 'Inga uppdrag'
                OR (person_data.status)::text = 'Avgången riksdagsledamot') AS inactive_members
           FROM public.person_data
          WHERE person_data.party IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY person_data.party
        ), party_violations AS (
         SELECT p.party,
            count(DISTINCT rv.id) AS total_violations,
            count(DISTINCT rv.reference_id) AS members_with_violations,
            max(rv.detected_date) AS latest_violation
           FROM public.person_data p
             JOIN public.rule_violation rv
               ON (rv.reference_id)::text = (p.id)::text
              AND (rv.resource_type)::text = 'POLITICIAN'
              AND (rv.status)::text = ANY (ARRAY['MINOR'::text, 'MAJOR'::text, 'CRITICAL'::text])
          WHERE p.party IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY p.party
        ), party_voting_annual AS (
         SELECT party,
            sum(total_votes) AS total_party_votes,
            avg(avg_percentage_absent) AS avg_absence_rate,
            avg(won_percentage) AS avg_win_rate,
            avg(rebel_percentage) AS avg_rebel_rate,
            avg(100::numeric - avg_percentage_absent) AS avg_participation_rate
           FROM public.view_riksdagen_vote_data_ballot_politician_summary_annual
          WHERE embedded_id_vote_date >= date_trunc('year', CURRENT_DATE - '1 year'::interval)
            AND embedded_id_vote_date < date_trunc('year', CURRENT_DATE::timestamp with time zone)
            AND party IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY party
        ), party_documents AS (
         SELECT p.party,
            count(DISTINCT vpd.id) AS documents_produced,
            count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN (vpd.document_type)::text = 'mot' THEN vpd.doc_id ELSE NULL END) AS motions_produced,
            count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN (vpd.document_type)::text = 'prop' THEN vpd.doc_id ELSE NULL END) AS propositions_produced
           FROM public.person_data p
             JOIN public.view_riksdagen_politician_document vpd
               ON (vpd.person_reference_id)::text = (p.id)::text
              AND vpd.made_public_date >= (CURRENT_DATE - '1 year'::interval)
          WHERE p.party IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY p.party
        ), party_government_roles AS (
         SELECT p.party,
            count(DISTINCT p.id) FILTER (WHERE (ad.assignment_type)::text = 'Departement') AS ministers_count,
            count(DISTINCT p.id) FILTER (WHERE (ad.assignment_type)::text = 'Riksdagsorgan'
                AND (ad.role_code)::text = 'Ordförande') AS committee_chairs_count
           FROM public.person_data p
             JOIN public.assignment_data ad ON (ad.intressent_id)::text = (p.id)::text
          WHERE (ad.to_date IS NULL OR ad.to_date >= CURRENT_DATE) AND p.party IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY p.party
        ), performance_calculations AS (
         SELECT pp.short_code AS party,
            pp.party_name,
            pp.registered_date AS registration_date,
            pmc.active_members, pmc.inactive_members,
            pv.total_violations, pv.members_with_violations, pv.latest_violation,
            pva.total_party_votes, pva.avg_absence_rate, pva.avg_win_rate,
            pva.avg_rebel_rate, pva.avg_participation_rate,
            pd.documents_produced, pd.motions_produced, pd.propositions_produced,
            pgr.ministers_count, pgr.committee_chairs_count,
            (COALESCE(pva.avg_participation_rate, 0) * 25 / 100.0
             + COALESCE(pva.avg_win_rate, 0) * 25 / 100.0
             + (100 - COALESCE(pva.avg_rebel_rate, 0)) * 20 / 100.0
             + LEAST(CASE WHEN COALESCE(pmc.active_members, 0::bigint) > 0
                         THEN COALESCE(pd.documents_produced, 0::bigint)::numeric / pmc.active_members::numeric
                         ELSE 0 END * 3, 15)
             + (15 - LEAST(COALESCE(pv.total_violations, 0::bigint)::numeric * 0.5, 15))
            ) AS calculated_performance_score
           FROM public.sweden_political_party pp
             LEFT JOIN party_member_counts pmc ON (pmc.party)::text = (pp.short_code)::text
             LEFT JOIN party_violations pv ON (pv.party)::text = (pp.short_code)::text
             LEFT JOIN party_voting_annual pva ON pva.party = (pp.short_code)::text
             LEFT JOIN party_documents pd ON (pd.party)::text = (pp.short_code)::text
             LEFT JOIN party_government_roles pgr ON (pgr.party)::text = (pp.short_code)::text
          WHERE pp.short_code IS NOT NULL
        )
 SELECT party, party_name, registration_date,
    COALESCE(active_members, 0::bigint) AS active_members,
    COALESCE(inactive_members, 0::bigint) AS inactive_members,
    COALESCE(total_violations, 0::bigint) AS total_violations,
    COALESCE(members_with_violations, 0::bigint) AS members_with_violations,
    CASE WHEN COALESCE(active_members, 0::bigint) > 0
         THEN round(COALESCE(members_with_violations, 0::bigint)::numeric / active_members::numeric * 100, 2)
         ELSE 0 END AS violation_rate_percentage,
    latest_violation AS latest_member_violation,
    COALESCE(total_party_votes, 0) AS total_votes_last_year,
    round(COALESCE(avg_absence_rate, 0), 2) AS avg_absence_rate,
    round(COALESCE(avg_win_rate, 0), 2) AS avg_win_rate,
    round(COALESCE(avg_rebel_rate, 0), 2) AS avg_rebel_rate,
    round(COALESCE(avg_participation_rate, 0), 2) AS avg_participation_rate,
    COALESCE(documents_produced, 0::bigint) AS documents_last_year,
    COALESCE(motions_produced, 0::bigint) AS motions_last_year,
    COALESCE(propositions_produced, 0::bigint) AS propositions_last_year,
    CASE WHEN COALESCE(active_members, 0::bigint) > 0
         THEN round(COALESCE(documents_produced, 0::bigint)::numeric / active_members::numeric, 2)
         ELSE 0 END AS docs_per_member,
    COALESCE(ministers_count, 0::bigint) AS current_ministers,
    COALESCE(committee_chairs_count, 0::bigint) AS current_committee_chairs,
    round(calculated_performance_score, 2) AS performance_score,
    CASE
        WHEN calculated_performance_score >= 75 THEN 'EXCELLENT'
        WHEN calculated_performance_score >= 60 THEN 'GOOD'
        WHEN calculated_performance_score >= 40 THEN 'AVERAGE'
        ELSE 'BELOW_AVERAGE'
    END AS performance_level,
    concat_ws(' | ',
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_participation_rate, 0) >= 85 THEN 'High participation' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_win_rate, 0) >= 60 THEN 'Effective voting' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_rebel_rate, 0) <= 5 THEN 'Strong discipline' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(documents_produced, 0::bigint) >= 50 THEN 'High productivity' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(ministers_count, 0::bigint) >= 1 THEN 'Government representation' ELSE NULL END
    ) AS strengths,
    concat_ws(' | ',
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_participation_rate, 0) < 80 THEN 'Low participation' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_win_rate, 0) < 50 THEN 'Low effectiveness' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(avg_rebel_rate, 0) > 10 THEN 'Discipline concerns' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(total_violations, 0::bigint) > 10 THEN 'Multiple violations' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN COALESCE(documents_produced, 0::bigint) < 10 THEN 'Low productivity' ELSE NULL END
    ) AS weaknesses
   FROM performance_calculations
  ORDER BY calculated_performance_score DESC

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity

INSERT INTO cia_view_row_counts(schemaname, viewname, view_type, row_count)
            VALUES (view_record.schemaname, view_record.object_name, view_record.object_type, row_count)

WITH RECURSIVE 
        -- Get all materialized view dependencies
        view_deps AS (
            SELECT DISTINCT
                dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
                source_table.relname AS depends_on,
                CASE 
                    WHEN source_table.relkind = $1 THEN $2
                    ELSE $3
                END AS depends_on_matview
            FROM pg_depend
            JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
            JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
            JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
            JOIN pg_namespace dependent_ns ON dependent_ns.oid = dependent_view.relnamespace
            JOIN pg_namespace source_ns ON source_ns.oid = source_table.relnamespace
            WHERE dependent_view.relkind = $4
              AND source_table.relkind IN ($5, $6, $7)
              AND pg_depend.deptype = $8
              AND dependent_ns.nspname = $9
              AND source_ns.nspname = $10
        ),
        -- Build dependency graph (only MV dependencies)
        mv_dependencies AS (
            SELECT view_name, depends_on
            FROM view_deps
            WHERE depends_on_matview = $11
        ),
        -- Get all materialized views
        all_mvs AS (
            SELECT matviewname AS view_name
            FROM pg_matviews
            WHERE schemaname = $12
        ),
        -- Calculate dependency depth using recursive CTE
        dependency_depth AS (
            -- Base case: views with no MV dependencies
            SELECT 
                am.view_name,
                $13 AS depth,
                ARRAY[am.view_name] AS path
            FROM all_mvs am
            WHERE NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT $14 FROM mv_dependencies md 
                WHERE md.view_name = am.view_name
            )
            
            UNION ALL
            
            -- Recursive case: views that depend on views we've already processed
            SELECT 
                md.view_name,
                dd.depth + $15,
                dd.path || md.view_name
            FROM mv_dependencies md
            JOIN dependency_depth dd ON md.depends_on = dd.view_name
            WHERE NOT (md.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
        ),
        -- Get maximum depth for each view
        max_depths AS (
            SELECT 
                view_name,
                MAX(depth) as max_depth
            FROM dependency_depth
            GROUP BY view_name
        )
        -- Output in refresh order
        SELECT 
            $16 AS schemaname,
            view_name AS matviewname,
            max_depth AS dependency_level
        FROM max_depths
        ORDER BY max_depth, view_name

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
      -- Skip views that timed out or had errors during Phase 1
      -- row_count: -1 = timeout, -2 = error, 0+ = success
      AND row_count >= $4
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL views, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE vc.row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty views
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($5, $6) THEN $7
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($8, $9, $10) THEN $11
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($12, $13, $14) THEN $15
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $16 THEN $17
            ELSE $18
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $19
      AND c.data_type IN ($20, $21, $22)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($23, $24) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $25::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($26,
                                   $27,
                                   $28) THEN
                LEAST($29::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $31 THEN 
                LEAST($32::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $34 THEN 
                LEAST($35::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $38 THEN 
                LEAST($39::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $40 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $41 THEN 
                LEAST($42::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $43 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $44 THEN 
                LEAST($45::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $47 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $48 THEN 
                LEAST($49::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($50::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $51::int THEN $52
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($53,
                                   $54,
                                   $55) THEN $56
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $57 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $58 THEN $59
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $60 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $61 THEN $62
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $64 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $65 THEN $66
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $67 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $68 THEN $69
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $70 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $71 THEN $72
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $73 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $74 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $75 THEN $76
            ELSE $77
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $78 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $79 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $80 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $81 || $82 ||
                $83 ||
                $84 ||
                $85 ||
                $86 ||
                $87 ||
                $88 ||
                $89 ||
                $90 ||
                $91 || $92 ||
                $93 || $94 || $95 || $96 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $97 THEN $98 || $99
                    ELSE $100 || $101
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $102 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $103 || $104 ||
                $105 ||
                $106 ||
                $107 ||
                $108 ||
                $109 ||
                $110 ||
                $111 ||
                $112 ||
                $113 || $114 ||
                $115 || $116 || $117 || $118 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $119 THEN $120 || $121
                    ELSE $122 || $123
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $124 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $125 || $126 ||
                $127 ||
                $128 ||
                $129 ||
                $130 ||
                $131 ||
                $132 ||
                $133 ||
                $134 ||
                $135 || $136 ||
                $137 || $138 || $139 || $140 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $141 THEN $142 || $143
                    ELSE $144 || $145
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $146 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $147 || $148 ||
                $149 ||
                $150 ||
                $151 ||
                $152 ||
                $153 ||
                $154 ||
                $155 ||
                $156 || $157 ||
                $158 || $159 || $160 || $161 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $162 THEN $163 || $164
                    ELSE $165 || $166
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $167 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $168 || $169 ||
                $170 ||
                $171 ||
                $172 ||
                $173 ||
                $174 ||
                $175 ||
                $176 ||
                $177 ||
                $178 || $179 ||
                $180 || $181 || $182 || $183 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $184 THEN $185 || $186
                    ELSE $187 || $188
                END,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $189 || $190 ||
                $191 || $192 ||
                $193 || $194 || $195 || $196 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $197 THEN $198 || $199
                    ELSE $200 || $201
                END,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $202 THEN $203
        WHEN $204 THEN $205
        WHEN $206 THEN $207
        WHEN $208 THEN $209
        WHEN $210 THEN $211
        ELSE $212
    END,
    viewname

SELECT * FROM (SELECT current_database() AS current_database, n.nspname,c.relname,a.attname,a.atttypid,a.attnotnull  OR (t.typtype = $4 AND t.typnotnull) AS attnotnull,a.atttypmod,a.attlen,t.typtypmod,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY a.attrelid ORDER BY a.attnum) AS attnum, nullif(a.attidentity, $5) as attidentity,nullif(a.attgenerated, $6) as attgenerated,pg_catalog.pg_get_expr(def.adbin, def.adrelid) AS adsrc,dsc.description,t.typbasetype,t.typtype  FROM pg_catalog.pg_namespace n  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class c ON (c.relnamespace = n.oid)  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attribute a ON (a.attrelid=c.oid)  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_type t ON (a.atttypid = t.oid)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attrdef def ON (a.attrelid=def.adrelid AND a.attnum = def.adnum)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description dsc ON (c.oid=dsc.objoid AND a.attnum = dsc.objsubid)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class dc ON (dc.oid=dsc.classoid AND dc.relname=$7)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace dn ON (dc.relnamespace=dn.oid AND dn.nspname=$8)  WHERE c.relkind in ($9,$10,$11,$12,$13) and a.attnum > $14 AND NOT a.attisdropped  AND n.nspname LIKE $1 AND c.relname LIKE $2) c WHERE $15  AND attname LIKE $3 ORDER BY nspname,c.relname,attnum

SELECT     tmp.TABLE_CAT AS "TABLE_CAT",     tmp.TABLE_SCHEM AS "TABLE_SCHEM",     tmp.TABLE_NAME AS "TABLE_NAME",     tmp.NON_UNIQUE AS "NON_UNIQUE",     tmp.INDEX_QUALIFIER AS "INDEX_QUALIFIER",     tmp.INDEX_NAME AS "INDEX_NAME",     tmp.TYPE AS "TYPE",     tmp.ORDINAL_POSITION AS "ORDINAL_POSITION",     trim(both $2 from pg_catalog.pg_get_indexdef(tmp.CI_OID, tmp.ORDINAL_POSITION, $3)) AS "COLUMN_NAME",   CASE tmp.AM_NAME     WHEN $4 THEN CASE tmp.I_INDOPTION[tmp.ORDINAL_POSITION - $5] & $6::smallint       WHEN $7 THEN $8       ELSE $9     END     ELSE $10   END AS "ASC_OR_DESC",     tmp.CARDINALITY AS "CARDINALITY",     tmp.PAGES AS "PAGES",     tmp.FILTER_CONDITION AS "FILTER_CONDITION",     tmp.REMARKS AS "REMARKS"FROM (SELECT current_database() AS TABLE_CAT,  n.nspname AS TABLE_SCHEM,   ct.relname AS TABLE_NAME, NOT i.indisunique AS NON_UNIQUE,   $11 AS INDEX_QUALIFIER, ci.relname AS INDEX_NAME,   CASE i.indisclustered     WHEN $12 THEN $13    ELSE CASE am.amname       WHEN $14 THEN $15      ELSE $16    END   END AS TYPE,   (information_schema._pg_expandarray(i.indkey)).n AS ORDINAL_POSITION,   ci.reltuples AS CARDINALITY,   ci.relpages AS PAGES,   pg_catalog.pg_get_expr(i.indpred, i.indrelid) AS FILTER_CONDITION,   ci.oid AS CI_OID,   i.indoption AS I_INDOPTION,   am.amname AS AM_NAME,   d.description AS REMARKS FROM pg_catalog.pg_class ct   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON (ct.relnamespace = n.oid)   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_index i ON (ct.oid = i.indrelid)   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class ci ON (ci.oid = i.indexrelid)   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_am am ON (ci.relam = am.oid)   LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description d ON (ci.oid = d.objoid) WHERE $17  AND ct.relname = $1) AS tmp ORDER BY "NON_UNIQUE", "TYPE", "INDEX_NAME", "ORDINAL_POSITION"
